resources.string: Only a food category without a corresponding sugar entry was added, as no complete information was entered for this.
Do you really want to delete the following category name?
This entry would breach your threshold of maximum grams per day
This entry would breach your threshold of maximum calories per day

The graph shows the last 35 entries (as far as available)

The red line represents the daily sugar gram limit that you can set in Settings.

The blue line connects the dots which represent daily sugar consumptions in total.

Daily gram values over 100g are too high to be represented on the graph.

This overview shows the course of the recorded sugar values per day (maximum 35 days).\n\nA green tick at the bottom left indicates that the set upper sugar limit was adhered to on that day. A warning symbol with a border around the card indicates that the upper limit has been exceeded.
Selectable today up to 30 days in the past
To monitor your daily sugar consumption by writing down the sugar consumed in grams. In addition, the calories consumed can be entered and recorded daily.
Can the app be used free of charge?
Yes, the app is completely free and no advertising is displayed.
Why does a user have to enter the sugar content of a particular food product?
Entering the sugar values by yourself should give you a better feeling for your own sugar consumption.
What do the two input variants \'Sugar/100g\' and \'Sugar/piece\' mean?

For the sugar/100g variant, the sugar content per 100g can be read off the packing and entered in the first field. The quantity consumed must also be entered. The sugar content is then calculated automatically. If the amount of sugar in a product is also known per piece, the sugar content per piece can be entered as well.
Do I have to log in to use the app?
No, the app can be used in full without logging in.
Is there an explanation of how to use the app?
Yes, in the settings under \'Introduction\' there is an explanation for each of the 4 main pages.
In which languages is the app currently available?
English, German and Spanish. The default language for devices in other languages is English.
Can the color scheme of the app be changed?
Yes, the color settings of the Android system can be activated.
What was the motivation behind the development of this app?
The motivation behind this app is not to exceed the upper limit of 50 grams of sugar per day recommended by many sources. This app is designed to help you achieve this goal.
Why is the corresponding food product with sugar information not automatically recognized when the barcode is scanned?
The idea of this app is that users deal with sugar and/or calorie values on their own. Therefore, sugar and/or calorie values are not loaded automatically.
Isn\'t it too time-consuming to enter everything that has a little sugar in this app?

It is up to you how you use the app. However, a common method would be to set the upper sugar limit (here in the app settings) 5-15 grams lower than the upper limit you are aiming for per day, so that there is room for small amounts and you remain below the desired amount of sugar per day even without entering them.
Select these values as your new daily limits?

There is no product category connected to this barcode number yet. If you fill out the other fields and save the entry the product name entered in the \'Category\' field is automatically assigned to this barcode number \'%1$s\'.
There is no entry for this category yet
The sugar and calorie entries can be saved in text files in CSV format. Two separate files are saved in the Downloads folder of your device.
Your data was successfully saved to the Downloads folder.
Unfortunately, the sugar entries could not be saved in a file. Please try again.
No entry data found for the given category
Data older than %1$s is automatically deleted on a monthly basis.

1) For which day should the entry be entered?

2) Link the entry to a barcode so that the sugar values are automatically entered for this category when the barcode is scanned again.

3) Enter here which food was eaten. The exact name can be chosen by the user. It can be a general name, a brand name or something similar.

1) The sugar value can be entered per 100g, as this information can usually be found on packaging. In addition, the amount of food consumed must be stated in grams.\n\n2) Or the sugar content is stated per piece, whereby the number can also be stated (by default 1)
1) By clicking ‘Save’, the information is written to the app\'s database.\n\n2) Today and yesterday are displayed on the first page.

1) The history of entries is displayed in the form of cards. One card contains the entries for one day.

2) 50g of chocolate were consumed here. The sugar content per 100g can be viewed by tapping on the food category. In this case, it is 50g per 100g, as 25g of sugar was calculated.

3) 3 pieces of wine gum were eaten here, each piece of which contains 3g of sugar.
If the upper limit entered in the settings has been exceeded, the corresponding tag is outlined in red and marked at the bottom left with the corresponding symbol.
The red horizontal line indicates the defined upper limit. The blue line indicates the course of consumption.

1) The search icon opens a letter bar, which can be used to jump to a specific letter.\n\n2) The categories can be deleted individually via the trash icon using the checkbox. Clicking on the icon again removes the delete view.
By clicking on a specific category, a window opens in which details of this category are listed.

1) In addition to recording the sugar values, calories can also be counted.

2)Daily upper limits are set for sugar in grams (g) and calories in kilocalories (kcal), which can be adjusted using the slider.

3) The recorded entries can be exported in CSV format.

4) In addition, automatic deletion of older entries can be activated.

5) The color scheme selected in the Android operating system settings can also be selected.
If you set this switch, the SugarCounter app will use the color scheme that you have set in the Android operating system in the settings.
This page is unfortunately not available in the landscape mode. Please change to the vertical format to view this page.
Enter sugar per one hundred gram here
Choose the date of your entry
Retrieve the details of a product category by scanning its barcode
Enter a value in grams under one hundred here
Enter a food category here or select one from the list
Enter a value in grams for one piece
